## Headroom Learned Patterns
*Auto-generated by `headroom learn` on 2026-07-15 — do not edit manually*

### Forge SWE Bench Workflow
*~18,588 tokens/session saved*
- For Flask 4045, use the bench checkout directly: `/home/floris/Documents/Repositories/Personal/AI/forge/.forge/swe-bench/pallets__flask-4045`; avoid repeatedly wrapping every shell probe in long `cd ... &&` commands. Batch related probes in one shell command or set the tool working directory when available.

### Python / Test Environment
*~12,000 tokens/session saved*
- Old Flask 4045 under Python 3.14 has unrelated compatibility failures (`_request_ctx_stack`, `pkgutil.get_loader`, conftest import issues); avoid full `pytest tests/test_blueprints.py` loops and run only the new/targeted test or a minimal import/assertion probe.
- Do not try system `pip install` in this environment: it is externally managed. Use existing deps and summarize environment blockers instead of retrying installs.

### RTK / Tool Output Guardrails
*~2,881 tokens/session saved*
- If reading `tests/test_blueprints.py` fails or returns a truncated/error payload, do not retry nearby `Read` slices; use `grep -n`, `wc -l`, or one `nl -ba tests/test_blueprints.py | sed -n '<range>p'` command to get exact lines.
- For `src/flask/blueprints.py`, read the relevant `Blueprint.__init__` region once (`~175-205`) or the whole file once; avoid repeated partial/full re-reads after the symbol location is known.
- Prefer relative paths inside the Flask 4045 checkout for `mcp__forge__read_file`; repeated absolute `.forge/swe-bench/...` reads waste context and often duplicate the same file content.

### Forge Harness
*~2,000 tokens/session saved*
- In Forge harness sessions, any web access must use `mcp__forge__web_search` and `mcp__forge__web_fetch`; do not use native/provider browsing tools.

<!-- headroom:learn:end -->
